body.title: Igor: curiosity from recognition, not just idle
body.result: curiosity_recognition.py: score_recognition() (pure Jaccard gap-fill), emit_curiosity_event() (TWM push), recognize_watchlist_gap() tool. Novelty: serendipitous(0.9) > gap_explanation(0.5–0.85) > confirmation(0.3). 18 tests.
body.intention: I intend that Igor emits proportional curiosity when incoming information resolves a watchlist gap — not just when idle.
body.description:
CuriositySource fires on idle/boredom, but the watchlist has no recognition mechanism: when Igor reads something that explains a gap, nothing amplifies that signal. The result is Igor 'never moves' even with working curiosity infrastructure. Two separate handles are needed: boredom (push from idle) vs. curiosity (pull toward unexplained gaps). The novelty of a hit matters: a confirmation (repeats known things) should produce a small signal; an explanation of a previously-open gap should produce a larger one; serendipitous discovery (explains something not known to be a gap) should produce the strongest signal.

Proposed shape: a recognition step in the watchlist processing path that scores incoming information against open watchlist questions by gap-fill value (not just keyword match), and emits a curiosity event weighted by novelty. Distinct from the idle-triggered CuriositySource.

**Affected files:** TBD — discovery step in sprint; likely devices/igor/cognition/push_sources.py (CuriositySource), devices/igor/tools/watchlist.py, possibly milieu.py for signal ingestion
**Design rules:** Igor uses systems (tools, habits, goals) — it doesn't contain them; no-sqlite
**Scope boundary:** In scope: recognition scoring in watchlist processing, curiosity event emission weighted by novelty. Out of scope: changing the boredom pathway, changing watchlist seeding, full self-directing behavior.
**Completion criteria:** When Igor processes an item that resolves a known watchlist gap, a curiosity event fires with measurably higher weight than a confirmation event. Observable via debug log or milieu state. Unit test: mock watchlist with one open question, feed it a gap-resolving item and a confirmation item, assert gap-resolver produces higher curiosity signal.
